articles.title: Gold: Safe-haven rally fades with rate repricing – Commerzbank
articles.snippet: Commerzbank’s Thu Lan Nguyen notes that Gold briefly surged above USD 5,400 per troy ounce yesterday, outperforming the Dollar and US Treasuries, before retreating back to Friday’s level.

articles.title: Oil: Supply shock risk grows with Hormuz shutdown – Danske Bank
articles.snippet: Danske Research Team highlights severe disruption to Oil and gas markets as conflict involving the United States, Israel and Iran escalates. Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has stalled, stranding significant volumes and driving freight rates sharply higher.

articles.title: Silver price today: Silver falls, according to FXStreet data
articles.snippet: Silver prices (XAG/USD) fell on Tuesday, according to FXStreet data. Silver trades at $84.81 per troy ounce, down 5.78% from the $90.01 it cost on Monday.

articles.title: USD/INR seems to extend gains on Wednesday amid US-Iran war
articles.snippet: The USD/INR pair ended Monday’s session 0.7% higher to near 92.00. The pair is expected to open higher on Wednesday, with Indian markets remaining closed on Tuesday due to Holi celebrations, as the US Dollar (USD) extends its gains.

articles.title: Forex Today: USD holds ground as Middle East chaos spreads
articles.snippet: Here is what you need to know on Tuesday, March 3:

articles.title: US-Iran war jeopardizes BoJ rate hike bets for March – Reuters
articles.snippet: Heightened market volatility due to escalating oil prices amid the war in the Middle East between the United States (US), Iran, and Israel has raised concerns over the likelihood of an interest rate hike by the Bank of Japan (BoJ) in its upcoming monetary policy meeting on March 19.

articles.title: Fed: Three cuts still expected – BNY
articles.snippet: BNY macro strategist John Velis maintains a forecast for three Federal Reserve rate cuts in the second half of the year despite geopolitical tensions and firmer producer prices.

articles.title: Gold weakens below $5,300 as sustained USD buying counter Middle East tensions
articles.snippet: Gold (XAU/USD) attracts some intraday selling on Tuesday and falls around $100 from the daily top, around the $5,380 area. The US Dollar (USD) climbs to a fresh high since January 20 and turns out to be a key factor exerting downward pressure on the commodity.

articles.title: USD: Safe-haven flows return with war risks – Rabobank
articles.snippet: Rabobank strategist Molly Schwartz highlights that the US Dollar’s strong performance reflects renewed safe-haven demand as geopolitical risks escalate around Iran.

articles.title: Gold price today, Tuesday, March 3: Gold price steadies with a stronger U.S. dollar in play
Rupee crashes 42 paise to settle at 91.50 against US dollar
articles.snippet: The rupee saw a steep loss of 42 paise to settle at 91.50 (provisional) against the US dollar on Monday after US and Israeli attacks on Iran intensified worldwide risk aversion, setting crude oil prices on fire and demand for the American currency soaring.

articles.title: US-Israel attack on Iran rattles rupee as markets feel heat
articles.snippet: The US and Israel’s combined attack on Iran had its impact on the Rupee, which opened about 16 paise weaker against the Dollar on Monday.

articles.title: Dogecoin (DOGE) Slips Toward Critical Support, Breakdown Threat Emerges
articles.snippet: Dogecoin started a recovery wave above the $0.0925 zone against the US Dollar. DOGE is now facing hurdles near $0.0970 and might struggle to continue higher. DOGE price started a recovery wave from $0.0880 and climbed above $0.0950. The price is trading below…

articles.title: Rupee falls 21 paise to 91.29 against US dollar in early trade
articles.snippet: The rupee falls 21 paise to 91.29 against the dollar amid rising crude prices and global market volatility.

articles.title: Dollar Surges as Traders Brace for War Impact: Markets Wrap - Bloomberg.com
articles.snippet: Dollar Surges as Traders Brace for War Impact: Markets WrapBloomberg.com Safe-haven yen and Swiss franc gain as weekend Iran strikes unnerve marketsReuters U.S. dollar seen strengthening as U.S.-Israel strikes intensifyInvesting.com Risk-sensative Aussie doll…
